questionText: A lemonade jug was 9/10 full before a game. Then 4/10 of the whole jug was poured into cups. Later, 1/10 of the whole jug was poured back in. Which statement is correct about whether the jug ended 6/10 full?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Fractions: Adding & Subtracting Fractions, aligned to Students can solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions.
options: [{"text":"Yes. The amount changes by -4/10 and then +1/10, so the net change is -3/10. Starting from 9/10, the jug ends at 6/10 full.","correct":true},{"text":"No. The jug ends at 5/10 full because once lemonade is poured out, the later 1/10 poured back should not be counted.","correct":false},{"text":"No. The jug ends at 4/10 full because both 4/10 and 1/10 are changes from the original 9/10 that should be subtracted.","correct":false},{"text":"No. The jug ends at 7/10 full because 4/10 poured out and 1/10 poured back means the jug lost only 2/10.","correct":false}]
explanation: Start with 9/10 full. Pouring out 4/10 gives 9/10 - 4/10 = 5/10. Pouring back 1/10 gives 5/10 + 1/10 = 6/10. Another way is to combine the changes: -4/10 + 1/10 = -3/10, and 9/10 - 3/10 = 6/10. So the jug did end 6/10 full. The 5/10 option ignores the amount poured back in. The 4/10 option wrongly subtracts the last change instead of adding it. The 7/10 option miscalculates the net change; losing 4/10 and gaining 1/10 is a loss of 3/10, not 2/10.

questionText: A toy box had 3/12 of a set of blocks inside. Then 5/12 more of the set was packed in. After that, some blocks were taken out, and the box ended with 6/12 of the set. Mia says, "Since the box ended at 6/12, 6/12 of the set must have been taken out." Which statement is correct?

explanation: First find how many blocks were in the box before any were taken out: 3/12 + 5/12 = 8/12. The box ended with 6/12, so the amount taken out was 8/12 - 6/12 = 2/12. questionText: A toy train moved 6/9 of the track, rolled back 2/9, then moved forward 4/9 more. Leo writes 6/9 + 4/9 - 2/9 and says his equation is wrong because he did not follow the story order. Which judgment is correct?
